mcqs.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of the Heapsort algorithm when sorting n elements?
mcqs.explanation: Heapsort always performs in O(n log n) time because both the heapify operation (O(log n)) is called n times, and building the heap takes O(n) time.

mcqs.question: In a max-heap represented as an array, if a parent element is stored at index i, at which index will its right child be stored?
mcqs.explanation: In array representation of a binary heap, the left child is at 2i+1 and the right child is at 2i+2 for a parent at index i.

mcqs.question: Consider the array [4, 1, 3, 9, 7]. After applying the build-max-heap procedure (using the Floyd's method), what will be the resulting array representation of the max-heap?
mcqs.explanation: Building a max-heap from [4, 1, 3, 9, 7] results in [9, 7, 3, 4, 1], where 9 is the root, 7 and 3 are its children, and 4 and 1 are the children of 7.

mcqs.question: In randomized quicksort, what is the primary advantage of choosing a random pivot element?

options.text: It always guarantees O(n log n) time complexity
It reduces the probability of worst-case partitioning O(n²)
It eliminates the need for recursion

mcqs.explanation: Random pivot selection ensures that the expected split is balanced, dramatically reducing the probability of encountering the O(n²) worst-case scenario.

mcqs.question: What is the expected time complexity of randomized quicksort when the pivot is chosen uniformly at random from the n elements?
mcqs.explanation: Randomized quicksort has an expected running time of O(n log n) because the random pivot ensures that on average, the array is divided into two roughly equal halves.

mcqs.question: Which of the following statements correctly distinguishes Monte Carlo algorithms from Las Vegas algorithms?

options.text: Monte Carlo algorithms always produce correct results, while Las Vegas algorithms may produce incorrect results with bounded error
Monte Carlo algorithms may produce incorrect results with bounded probability, while Las Vegas algorithms always produce correct results but with random running time
Both always produce correct results but differ in speed

mcqs.explanation: Monte Carlo algorithms have deterministic running time but may give wrong answers with bounded error, while Las Vegas algorithms always give correct results but their runtime is random.

mcqs.question: The randomized selection algorithm (Rand-Select) can find the i-th smallest element in expected O(n) time. What is its worst-case time complexity?
mcqs.explanation: In the worst case, randomized selection can have O(n²) time complexity if consistently unlucky with pivot selections, though this probability is extremely low.

mcqs.question: A company needs to process a large dataset to find the median element. They have two options: (i) sort the entire array using Heapsort and pick the middle element, or (ii) use randomized selection. Which statement is TRUE regarding the practical choice?

options.text: Heapsort is always faster because it guarantees O(n log n)
Randomized selection is always faster because it has expected O(n) time
For a single median finding, randomized selection is typically faster despite worst-case O(n²), while Heapsort is preferable when sorting is needed anyway

mcqs.explanation: Randomized selection is expected linear time and faster for just median finding, but Heapsort guarantees O(n log n) and is better when multiple order statistics are needed or when sorting is required.
